items.title: Lt Gen Aamer Raza promoted to general, appointed as Commander of National Strategic Command
items.summary:
Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Friday promoted Chief of the General Staff Lieutenant General Aamer Raza to the rank of four-star general and appointed him as Commander of the National Strategic Command.
The prime minister congratulated General Aamer on his promotion and new assignment, expressing confidence in his ability to discharge the responsibilities of the key strategic post.
PM Shehbaz al

items.title: ADNOC offers crude oil in 7th tender issued since June, sources say
items.summary:
SINGAPORE: Abu Dhabi National Oil Co is offering crude in a tender, its seventh issued since the start of June, even as shipping disruptions and fighting in the Middle East have escalated, trade sources said on Friday.
ADNOC offered crude loading from August to October.
The producer added that any bid from buyers at a discounted differential would be automatically rejected, the sources said.
Bid s

items.title: Thai baht/US dollar steady on Friday
items.summary:
BANGKOK: The Thai baht was little changed against the US dollar on Friday.
At 0307 GMT, the baht was 0.01% lower at 33.82 versus the dollar, after trading in a range of 33.805 to 33.840.
It ended the previous session at 33.81 per dollar, as per LSEG data.

items.title: JGB yields rise sharply as weak yen fuels bets for BOJ rate hike
items.summary:
TOKYO: Japanese government bond yields rose sharply on Friday as the weak yen and rising oil prices fuelled bets that the Bank of Japan would raise its interest rates earlier.
The two-year yield, most sensitive to BOJ’s policy rates, rose 2 basis points to 1.51%, its highest level since May 1995.
The 30-year yield, which reflects inflation worries, rose to as high as 4%, its highest since July 9,

items.title: Yuan set for flat week as oil-led dollar strength caps gains
items.summary:
HONG KONG: China’s yuan traded in a narrow range against the US dollar on Friday as investors grappled with renewed concerns over hostilities in the Middle East and rising oil prices.
 The dollar rode US Treasury yields higher on Friday after US President Donald Trump threatened “major military punishment” for Iran and its Houthi allies.
Brent crude climbed back above $100 a barrel for the first t

items.title: India youth protest leaders to hold talks with government but protests will continue
items.summary:
NEW DELHI: Leaders of India’s youth-led “cockroach” movement will meet the government on Friday to try and resolve a face-off over national examination paper leaks although they will not halt nationwide protests, a spokesperson for the group said.
The decision was announced hours after activist Sonam Wangchuk ended a 26-day hunger strike overnight, raising hopes that the two sides could be moving

items.title: China braces for heavy rain as tropical storm Noul edges closer
items.summary:
HONG KONG: China on Friday warned of heavy rain and severe flood risks as the third tropical cyclone this month tracked closer to the country’s southern coast, where it is expected to intensify over the weekend.
As of Friday morning, Noul was northeast of Manila, the capital of the Philippines, moving west-northwest at around 25 to 30 kph (16 to 19 mph).
The tropical storm is forecast to move with

items.title: US energy firm eyes Balochistan minerals, oil-gas projects in talks with Pakistan
items.summary:
Federal Minister for Petroleum Ali Pervaiz Malik held separate meetings with representatives of leading international energy companies to discuss investment opportunities in Pakistan’s energy and critical minerals sectors.
In the first meeting, the minister met with Gregory Bloom, President of Morgan Hughes Energy, a US-based energy company, and Ahmad Waleed, CEO of Tejara Capital. The discussions

items.title: Copper heads for weekly rise despite Mideast pullback
items.summary:
SINGAPORE: Copper was set to end the week higher despite coming off mid-week highs, with an escalation in the Middle East war worsening inflation fears and weighing on the macroeconomic outlook for the global economy.
Benchmark three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange held firm, adding 0.11% at $13,609.5 a metric ton by 0300 GMT.
The most-traded copper contract on the Shanghai Futures Excha

items.title: Swiss government takes note of new US tariffs
items.summary:
ZURICH: Switzerland takes note of the announced additional US tariffs of up to 12.5% on Swiss imports, effective July 24, the government said on Friday.
The United States is thereby still adhering to a joint statement of November 2025, the Swiss statement added.
Pakistan among countries hit with Trump’s new 10% US forced labour tariff

items.title: China stocks slip as oil price surge outweighs market support efforts
items.summary:
HONG KONG: China and Hong Kong stocks retreated on Friday on higher oil prices, fuelling inflation fears and dampening investor sentiment.
China’s blue-chip CSI300 Index and the Shanghai Composite Index fell 1.2% by lunch break.  Hong Kong benchmark Hang Seng dropped 1.7%, while Hang Seng Tech lost 1.7%.
Brent crude jumped to above $100 a barrel after US President Donald Trump promised “major mili

items.title: Thailand car production falls 7.55% in June, full-year forecast cut
items.summary:
BANGKOK: Car production in Thailand fell in June, down 7.55% from a year earlier to 120,391 units, the Federation of Thai Industries said on Friday.
The reading follows a year-on-year drop in May of 11.43%.
Domestic car sales rose 17.26% year on year, after an annual increase of 10.60% in May, the Federation said.
June exports dropped 7.45% annually after a fall of 26.69% in May, according to the

items.title: South Korean shares sink 6% on AI spending concerns, oil spike
items.summary:
South Korean shares tumbled 6% on Friday on concerns over the sustainability of heavy AI spending by major US technology firms, while a surge in oil prices above $100 a barrel rekindled fears of a fresh inflation shock.
The benchmark KOSPI was down 6.3% at 6,650.41 points, as of 0431 GMT, erasing most gains from the previous three sessions when the index climbed roughly 9%.
The sharp decline, the

items.title: China iron ore imports set to rise this year as steelmakers make up for declining grade
items.summary:
China’s iron ore imports are set to rise for a third consecutive year in 2026, as steelmakers buy more to compensate for declining iron content and as Guinea’s Simandou project boosts supply, analysts said.
The top iron ore consumer’s imports are likely to climb as much as 4% to a record of just above 1.3 billion metric tons, analysts said, helping prevent any oversupply-induced price drop.
The wo

items.title: Oil shock, Treasury yield spike put Indian bonds under pressure before debt sale
items.summary:
MUMBAI: Indian government bonds slipped into the red at the start of a fourth straight session on Friday, as oil prices climbed above the $100-per-barrel mark and the 10-year US Treasury yield hit an 18-month high, souring sentiment ahead of a fresh debt sale.
The benchmark 6.94% 2036 bond yield traded at 6.8571% as of 10:30 a.m. IST, after closing at 6.8413% on Thursday.
Earlier in the session th

items.title: France requests help from EU to deal with fires, says Macron
items.summary:
PARIS: France has requested assistance from the European Union to help it deal with a major wildfire near the country’s Atlantic coast, said President Emmanuel Macron on Friday.
“The situation remains extremely tense with the fires hitting our country, particularly in Gironde,” wrote Macron on X.
“France has requested the activation of the European Union’s civil protection mechanism.
We will soon

items.title: EU's Kallas questions US rationale for tariffs on bloc, seeks clarification
items.summary:
MANILA: European Union foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas questioned Washington’s rationale for imposing new tariffs on European goods, saying on Friday that allegations of shortcomings in the bloc’s forced labour controls were unfounded.
“You can’t say that for the European Union,” Kallas told Reuters on the sidelines of ASEAN meetings in Manila.
“If you compare our labour laws to the ones of the U

items.title: Pakistan-origin Saadia Zahidi named first woman to head IATA
items.summary:
Pakistan-born executive Saadia Zahidi has been appointed as the next Director General of the International Air Transport Association (IATA), becoming the first woman to lead the global airline industry body when she assumes office on November 1, 2026.
Zahidi will be IATA’s ninth Director General and will join the aviation organisation from the World Economic Forum (WEF), where she is a Managing Di

items.title: Palm hits 15-week high on oil and Dalian strength, eyes third weekly gain
items.summary:
JAKARTA: Malaysian palm oil futures rose to a 15-week high on Friday, supported by strong oil prices and a rebound in Dalian’s palm olein futures,and were on course for a third straight weekly gain.
The benchmark palm oil contract for October delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange was up 41 ringgit, or 0.87%, at 4,751 ringgit ($1,161.33) a metric ton By the midday break.
The contract

items.title: Volkswagen revises down 2026 sales forecast after Q2 profit slump
items.summary:
BERLIN: Volkswagen revised down its sales forecast for 2026 on Friday, now expecting a decline of up to 3%, after its operating profit slumped in the second quarter.
The German auto group, which includes subsidiaries Porsche and Audi, reported a lower-than-forecast operating profit of €3.5 billion ($3.98 billion) in the April-to-June period, down 9.5% year on year.

items.title: Gold softens on prospects of Fed rate hikes as Brent tops $100
items.summary:
Gold extended losses on Friday after falling 2% in the previous session, as Brent crude’s move back above $100 a barrel stoked inflation concerns and strengthened the case for higher interest rates ahead of a Federal Reserve policy meeting next week.
Spot gold eased 0.5% to $4,027.54 per ounce by 0523 GMT, with prices down more than $130 from a two-week high hit on Wednesday.

items.title: Indian stars Ranbir Kapoor and Yash at Comic-Con to promote 'Ramayana'
items.summary:
LOS ANGELES: Indian actors Ranbir Kapoor and Yash arrived at San Diego Comic-Con on Thursday to promote “Ramayana,” a two-part film adaptation of one ​of Hinduism’s most revered epics.
Kapoor, who plays Prince Rama, said appearing at ‌the convention was a significant moment for the production as it seeks to introduce the ancient story to a global audience.
“I’ve always been a fan of comic conventi

items.title: 'Avatar Aang' film creators unveil first Avatar Studios film at Comic-Con despite setbacks
items.summary:
LOS ANGELES: “Avatar: ​The Last Airbender” creators Michael Dante DiMartino and Bryan Konietzko unveiled their latest project, animated fantasy film “Avatar ‌Aang: The Last Airbender,” at San Diego Comic-Con on Thursday, saying they wanted their first Avatar Studios project to resonate with longtime fans.
“We had a lot of ideas for different projects in TV and film,” DiMartino told Reuters. “The i

items.title: Pakistan among countries hit with Trump's new 10% US forced labour tariff
items.summary:
WASHINGTON: The Trump administration on Friday imposed new tariffs of 10% and 12.5% on goods from 60 trading partners, including Pakistan, the European Union and China, over allegations of lax enforcement of forced labour bans, just as a temporary 10% global tariff expired.
The move is the White House’s latest effort to restore President Donald Trump’s campaign vision of a near-global tariff after

items.title: Service Industries announces new tyre subsidiary for business expansion
items.summary:
Service Industries Limited (SIL) announced on Friday that its wholly owned subsidiary Service Tyres (Private) Limited (STPL) intended to incorporate a wholly owned subsidiary under the name Service Tyres International (Private) Limited for expansion of tyre business.
The company announced via a notice to the Pakistan Stock Exchange today.
The notice stated that additional information regarding the

items.title: Selling intensifies at PSX as Middle East tensions escalate
items.summary:
Selling pressure persisted at the Pakistan Stock Exchange (PSX) as escalating tensions in the Middle East took their toll on investor sentiments, with the benchmark KSE-100 Index shedding over 1,800 points during the opening minutes of trading on Friday.
At 9:48am, the benchmark index was hovering at 169,885.48, down by 1,853.96  points or 1.08%.
Selling was observed in key sectors, including auto

items.title: US unveils new tariffs on 60 trade partners, including Pakistan, over forced labour concerns
items.summary:
The United States said on Thursday that it would impose new tariffs on 60 trading partners, including Pakistan, India, China and the European Union, over forced labour concerns, replacing an expiring global duty rolled out by US President Donald Trump earlier this year.
The levies, effective Friday, range from 10 per cent to 12.5pc.
“The United States has had a forced labor import ban for nearly a

items.title: Aurangzeb for deeper partnership with US
items.summary:
WASHINGTON: Finance Mi­­n­ister Muhammad Aurangzeb’s three-day visit, which ended ear­ly on Thursday, highlights Islamabad’s attempt to move Pakistan’s economic relationship with the US beyond the familiar cycle of financial crises, IMF programmes and emergency support.
The visit produced no immediate announcement of a major financial package, but it opened discussions on several fronts — from IMF

items.title: Community nutrition project: PKNC reviews progress
items.summary: FAISALABAD: The 3rd meeting of the Scientific Advisory Board (SAB) of Pak-Korea Nutrition Center (PKNC) was chaired by Prof. Dr. Zulfiqar Ali, Vice Chancellor, University of Agriculture Faisalabad (UAF), to review the progress of the project and reaffirm the collective commitment of academia, development partners, and policymakers towards combating malnutrition and promoting community nutrition ac

items.title: Pak­istan Medical Association warns of ‘shutdown’ over FBR’s tax system for clinics
items.summary:
ISLAMABAD: The Pak­istan Medical Association (PMA) has opposed the Federal Board of Reve­nu­e’s (FBR) proposed mandatory Point of Sale (POS) integration for doctors’ clinics, warning that healthcare facilities across the country could be shut down if the tax authority did not withdraw what it described as “bureaucratic overreach”.
The warning comes days after the FBR issued a notification directin

items.title: Japan June core inflation accelerates, stays below BOJ target
items.summary:
TOKYO: Japan’s core inflation perked ​up in June but remained below the central bank’s 2% target for a fifth straight month, suggesting ‌that firms have yet to aggressively pass on rising input costs to households.
But analysts expect consumer inflation to accelerate later this year as the recent surge in producer prices, driven by rising fuel and import costs from the Middle East conflict and the

items.title: Azma’s rejoinder to PPP leaders
items.summary:
LAHORE: Punjab Minister for Information and Culture Azma Bokhari has responded to recent statements by Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP) spokespersons, saying that everyone knows the reason behind the party’s hostility toward Punjab.
She said public funds in Punjab are being spent on the welfare of the people and development projects, and this is precisely what appears to be troubling the PPP.
Azma Bok

items.title: CM for utilising all resources to deal with urban flooding challenge
items.summary:
LAHORE: Punjab Chief Minister Maryam Nawaz Sharif on Thursday directed all relevant departments to utilise every available human and mechanical resource to effectively deal with urban flooding, hill torrents and possible flood emergencies across the province.
“People are losing their lives because of the negligence of power distribution companies. Dangling electric wires on utility poles have beco

items.title: UK eyes strong trade ties with Pakistan
items.summary:
ISLAMABAD: The United Kingdom is examining the possibility of transforming trade dialogue into a stronger institutional mechanism with clearly defined sectors, working groups and implementation timelines.

This was stated by British High Commissioner Jane Marriott during a meeting with Commerce Minister Jam Kamal here on Thursday. The meeting reviewed various proposals to increase trade between th

items.title: PSX plunges 2,690 points amid oil upsurge
items.summary: KARACHI: Panic-stricken investors continued to unload their positions as the Gulf war kept fuelling apprehensions about the energy supply scenario, stoking fears of an upsurge in inflationary pressures, especially in Pakistan, which is reliant on imports via the Strait of Hormuz. Consequently, the Pakistan Stock Exchange (PSX) on Thursday extended the bearish trend, dragging the benchmark KSE-100

items.title: Oil price tops $100 as supply disruption worsens
items.summary:
NEW YORK: Oil prices topped $100 on Thursday, their highest levels in nearly two months, after Yemen’s Houthis said they had attacked two Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea, causing further global supply disruptions following a near-halt in trade through the Strait of Hormuz.

Brent futures rose $6.59, or 7 per cent, to $100.66 a barrel at 12:58pm ET (1658 GMT), exceeding $100 for the first time sin

items.title: KE industrial power hits record
items.summary:
KARACHI: K-Elec­tr­ic’s industrial feeders’ consumption reached a record high of 6.08 billion units in FY26, indicating consistent electricity supply as the national economy grew by 3.7pc.

Chief Executive Syed Muhammad Taha said that the utility has provided industries with loadshedding-exempt power supply since 2013 and would continue to support that growth through reliable and sustainable power
